meaning: adj.<罕>苍白的,可怕的,血红的,耸人听闻的
examples: She took care to paint her toe nails a lurid red or orange.
It was whispered, by those who peered after her, that the scarlet letter threw a lurid gleam along the dark passageway of the interior.
The trio make lurid allegations about specific incidents.
Her narrative, though sometimes tangential and disjointed, rattles enjoyably through a lurid and restless landscape.
The result is part trip, part secret journal, spliced together into a lurid public memorandum.
